exemplar.title: Â¿En que medida tiene valor emancipatorio el concepto de placer en El Nacimiento del Placer de Carol Giligan?

detailedExemplarData.title: Â¿En que medida tiene valor emancipatorio el concepto de placer en El Nacimiento del Placer de Carol Giligan?

strengths: The research question is clearly stated, tightly focused and consistently connected to each analytical section.
Topic and key concepts are communicated accurately and effectively, with comprehensive explanations that guide a non-specialist reader.
The introduction and conclusion both revisit and frame the investigation clearly, demonstrating coherent focus throughout.
weaknesses: Methodology shows evidence of informed selection but is too narrow, relying almost exclusively on the primary text without justification for secondary choices.
Some definitions (e.g. placer vs. felicidad) are repetitive or vague, slightly detracting from flawless communication.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates an accurate and effective communication of topic and research question (band 5â6), but the methodology is limited in range (band 3â4). Overall performance sits at the top of the 3â6 range without achieving flawless precision, justifying a score of 5.

strengths: Demonstrates clear and coherent understanding of Gilliganâs ethics of care and the multidimensional concept of emancipation.
Appropriate use of key subject-specific terminology in most places (e.g. reciprocidad, autonomÃ­a).
Well-integrated quotations and case examples ground theoretical discussion.
weaknesses: Heavy dependence on a single primary source limits breadth and depth of perspective.
Key terms such as âplacerâ are not consistently differentiated from adjacent concepts like âfelicidad,â weakening analytical precision.
Claims about patriarchy and emancipation occasionally lack secondary scholarly support.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Source application is mostly relevant but narrow (band 3â4), knowledge is clear but not fully sophisticated (band 3â4), and terminology use is mostly accurate with occasional lapses (band 3â4). The overall profile aligns with the lower half of the 3â4 band, so a score of 3 is appropriate.

strengths: Provides a balanced analysis with coherent conclusions that acknowledge both strengths and weaknesses of Gilliganâs concept.
Discussion identifies tensions (individual vs collective emancipation) and evaluates economic and political dimensions effectively.
Illustrative examples are used persuasively to support the sustained argument.
weaknesses: Research quality is adequate but limited in diversity; key counter-arguments from other feminist ethicists are not engaged.
Some analytical leaps lack full justification and certain promised dimensions (e.g. psychological) are under-explored.
Critical evaluation is present but uneven, with counter-positions asserted rather than fully analysed.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research relevance fits the lower 4â6 band (score 4), analysis and conclusions are good (score 5), and discussion shows partially critical evaluation in the 7â9 band (score 7). Balancing these strands yields an overall mid-band performance, meriting 5 out of 12.

strengths: Clearly appropriate overall structure with consistent headings and a comprehensive table of contents.
Basic layout conventions (word count, title page, headings) are correctly applied.
Bibliography style is recognisable and hyperlinks are functional.
weaknesses: Abstract is missing and page numbering is inconsistent or absent.
Citation formatting is mixed (footnotes and inline), and heading numbering is overly complex in places.
Paragraph spacing and indentation vary, slightly disrupting readability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Most required elements of structure and layout are present and correctly applied (band 3â4), but omissions (abstract, page numbers) and inconsistencies warrant a lower-band placement, so a score of 3 is appropriate.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The research question â âÂ¿En quÃ© medida tiene valor emancipatorio el concepto de placerâ¦?â â is **precise, manageable and fully aligned** with the philosophical discussion that follows.  

The student keeps the investigation tightly focused on this question, structuring the main body around limits, potentials and ambiguities of emancipatory value. Each analytical sub-section explicitly reconnects with the wording of the question, showing a strong thread of relevance.  

Minor drifting occurs when long narrative summaries of Gilliganâs case studies overshadow direct argumentation, so absolute sharpness is not maintained throughout; nevertheless the question is clearly formulated, well focused and effectively drives the discussion.

Philosophical and feminist terminology such as âpatriarcadoâ, âautonomÃ­aâ, âreciprocidadâ, âÃ©tica del cuidadoâ and âemancipaciÃ³nâ is **used appropriately and mostly accurately**. The student shows awareness of distinctions between economic, psychological, personal and political emancipation.  

Nonetheless, some key terms (e.g. âplacerâ) are treated inconsistently, and occasional phrasing (âser inmensamente liberadaâ, ârol de mujerâ) lacks analytic precision. There are also minor lapses into everyday rather than technical usage, slightly weakening conceptual clarity.  

The performance is therefore typical of the 3â4 band, meriting a band score of 3.

The student states the research focus explicitly in both the title and introduction, then restates it throughout the paper when framing each section of analysis.  

â¢  A clear overview of Gilliganâs concept of pleasure, the ethics of care, and the emancipatory theme is given before the investigation begins, allowing readers to understand precisely **what will be examined and why it matters**.  
â¢  Explanations of key background ideas (placer, Ã©tica del cuidado, emancipaciÃ³n) are sufficiently detailed to orient a non-specialist reader, and the table of contents mirrors that explanatory structure.  

While thorough, some definitions are repetitive and there is minor vagueness in places (e.g. no explicit differentiation between âplacerâ and âfelicidadâ), so the communication is not flawless; nevertheless it is accurate, purposeful and essentially comprehensive, meriting the 5â6 band.

Basic layout conventions are respected: a word count is stated, a title page and table of contents are provided, and headings are consistently formatted. The bibliography uses a recognisable style and hyperlinks are included.  

Nevertheless, finer aspects show lapsesâthere is no evidence of page numbering in the excerpt, citation formatting is inconsistent (mix of footnotes and inline references), and the abstract is omitted.  

Given that most, though not all, layout elements are correctly applied, the submission meets the descriptors for the 3â4 band at the lower level (score 3).

The essay follows the required EE structureâtitle page with word count, abstract substitutes (brief intro), table of contents, numbered sections for introduction, development, conclusion and bibliographyâfacilitating coherent navigation. Paragraphing is logical and headers are used effectively.  

Some minor issues are visible: the abstract is absent, occasional subsection numbering is inconsistent (e.g., 2.2.2.3), and long blocks of quotation run over several pages, slightly disrupting flow.  

Despite these flaws, the structure remains clearly appropriate and meets the main requirements, placing the work in the 3â4 band at a score of 3.

The essay presents an extended discussion that identifies tensions (e.g., individual vs collective emancipation) and evaluates Gilliganâs thesis against economic, political and psychological criteria. There is a clear attempt to **develop a sustained argument** and to question the adequacy of pleasure as an emancipatory tool.  

Evaluation is presentâlimitations of essentialism and structural change are explicitly acknowledgedâand the student suggests directions for further research. Yet the critical edge is uneven: certain counter-positions are asserted rather than fully analysed and engagement with alternative philosophical perspectives is limited.  

This represents a solid âgood argument with partially critical evaluation,â matching the 7â9 band; the lower end score of 7 reflects the intermittently superficial treatment of counter-arguments.

Methodology is described in the introduction: close reading of Gilliganâs primary text, comparison with one conference talk and âother works by the authorâ.  

â¢  These choices are relevant but the **range is narrow**, consisting almost exclusively of Gilliganâs own material with only a handful of secondary items.  
â¢  There is evidence of some informed selection (primary source identified, word-count respected, theoretical framework outlined), yet no clear justification for why particular secondary sources were chosen or how alternative viewpoints were sought.  

Consequently the work shows a generally adequate but limited methodological foundation, corresponding to the 3â4 band; a mid-band score of 3 is appropriate.

The investigation is built on consistent engagement with Gilliganâs primary text and one supporting lecture, ensuring that the research remains **largely relevant to the stated question**.  

However, the quantity and diversity of research are limited: only three secondary sources are listed, two of which are popular or tertiary. Key scholarly counter-arguments in feminist ethics are not consulted. This restricts the breadth and depth of inquiry and leaves some claims insufficiently triangulated.  

Consequently the work demonstrates adequate but not extensive research that is mostly relevant to the question, aligning with the 4â6 band at its lower boundary (score 4).

The essay exhibits **clear and generally coherent understanding** of Gilliganâs ethics of care, feminist theory and the multidimensional notion of emancipation. Key ideas such as reciprocity, patriarchy, and autonomy are articulated accurately and linked to textual evidence.  

The student occasionally ventures beyond exposition to relate Gilliganâs views to broader feminist concerns (essentialism, collective action). Yet the discussion tends to remain within Gilliganâs framework, with only light reference to alternative positions, limiting depth.  

Given the solidity of core knowledge but partial dependence on a single thinker, the work fits the 3â4 band, and a score of 4 recognises the generally strong â though not fully sophisticated â grasp of subject matter.

The student structures the analysis around limitations, potentials and ambiguities, weighing evidence from the text against emancipatory criteria. This yields **reasonably balanced and coherent conclusions** that acknowledge both strengths and weaknesses of Gilliganâs concept.  

Illustrative examples (friendship, romantic partnerships, parentâchild relations) are examined, and conclusions about economic and political dimensions are logically inferred, albeit with modest external support. Some arguments remain descriptive and occasional leaps are made without full justification, showing partial rather than thorough support.  

Overall, the analysis is good and conclusions are generally supported, satisfying the 4â6 band; a mid-band score of 5 is justified.

Quotations and paraphrases from *El Nacimiento del Placer* are frequent and well integrated, demonstrating relevance to the points being argued. The student also cites the conference video and a small number of web sources.  

However, the **heavy dependence on a single primary text** means the range of perspectives is modest, and several claims (e.g. about patriarchy or emancipation theory) are asserted without external support. In places summaries of Gilliganâs anecdotes replace deeper engagement with broader philosophical literature.  

Overall, source use is mostly relevant and appropriate but remains limited in breadth and occasionally descriptive, placing it solidly in the 3â4 band at the lower end.

annotations.comment: The nesting of headings (e.g., 2.2.2.3) is overly complex; simplifying the numbering hierarchy would improve clarity.
The research question is stated clearly and concisely, providing a precise focus for the investigation.
The connection drawn between emotional autonomy and emancipatory potential is persuasive and supported by examples.
The introduction to the analysis section clearly outlines the plan and maintains alignment with the research question.
Repeating the research question verbatim in the narrative adds redundancy; focus on advancing original analysis rather than restating.
The economic and political limitations are analyzed coherently, showing good integration of theory and critique.
Despite promising a psychological dimension, that limit is not discussed here; ensure promised aspects are fully addressed.
The quote illustrating Irisâs paradox is well-chosen and anchors the psychological analysis effectively.
Pagination is absent on this page, undermining consistency; ensure page numbers appear in the footer.
Page numbering is missing from the footer, which impedes navigation and consistency across the document.
Long quotations dominate this section; concisely paraphrase key points and reserve direct quotes for pivotal statements.
Claims about patriarchyâs reaction to female pleasure would benefit from secondary scholarly support beyond Gilliganâs text.
The bibliography includes diverse source types (monograph, lecture, online review), reflecting an attempt to draw on varied material.
The stated word count of 3998 demonstrates compliance with the maximum-word requirement and is clearly displayed.
The table of contents is comprehensive and mirrors the structure of the investigation, facilitating reader navigation.
An abstract is not provided; including a concise summary of aims, methodology, and conclusions would strengthen the introduction and meet structural expectations.
The introduction describes personal interest but could situate the study more rigorously within broader scholarly debates to demonstrate informed selection of sources.
The title is presented clearly and accurately frames the investigationâs scope, satisfying the requirement for the reportâs title at the start. Well done on this formatting element.
The methodology outlines primary and secondary sources but lacks justification for selecting specific conference talks and web sources; explain why these were chosen.
The theoretical framework provides a detailed explanation of Gilliganâs concept of pleasure, demonstrating clear and coherent subject knowledge.
The term âplacerâ is not differentiated clearly from adjacent concepts like âfelicidadâ; refining definitions will enhance conceptual precision.
Footnote formatting interrupts the flow; consider consolidating citations in a consistent style (e.g., endnotes or in-text references).
The analysis relies heavily on Gilliganâs primary text with limited engagement of secondary literature; integrating broader perspectives will deepen the investigation.
The use of empirical examples from Gilliganâs case studies is effective and helps ground abstract concepts in real-world scenarios.
Repeated use of âibidemâ without clear first citation context may confuse readers; provide full references for each source.
The section on ethics of care links emotional and rational dimensions persuasively, showing effective use of subject-specific terminology.
The discussion does not engage alternative feminist ethics beyond Gilligan; including counter-arguments from other scholars would strengthen critical evaluation.
Citation style varies between inline references and footnotes; adopt a single consistent format throughout.
The multi-dimensional treatment of emancipation (economic, psychological, personal, political) demonstrates clear conceptual understanding.
An incomplete sentence breaks coherence at the page break; review continuity and ensure each section is complete.
Page numbers are not visible on this page, hindering navigation; verify footer settings.
This section revisits earlier theoretical summaries without extending the analysis; focus on novel insights to advance your argument.
Paragraph spacing and indentation are inconsistent here, which disrupts readability; apply uniform layout conventions.
The discussion of ambiguity in the term âplacerâ reflects critical engagement and deepens conceptual evaluation.
You note ambiguity around âplacerâ but do not provide your own operational definition; clarifying your usage would improve precision.
The conclusion succinctly revisits key findings and maintains alignment with the research question, demonstrating coherent focus.
The conclusion would benefit from explicitly acknowledging study limitations and proposing targeted future research questions.
References vary in format and completeness; adopt a consistent citation style (e.g., APA) and include full details for all entries.
The report lacks a dedicated reflection on research planning and decision-making; consider adding an evaluative section on challenges and methodological choices.
